content.text:
<user_query>
Workspace: C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq.
Design a deterministic extraction plan for fields: business_name, description, industry, main_products_services, target_market, unique_value_proposition, competitive_advantages, competitors, competitor_analysis, market_position, abn, phone, email, address, social_handles, trust_signals, executive_summary, confidence, cmo_executive_brief, seo_analysis, paid_media_analysis, social_media_analysis, website_health, swot, competitor_swot, cmo_priority_actions.
Include regex/string-rule strategy, unknown-marking rules, source evidence mapping format per field, ai_errors format, and innovation modules that remain evidence-bound. Return implementation checklist and pseudocode only.
</user_query>
Scanning the codebase for existing field names and extraction patterns so the plan aligns with your project.

Here is a **deterministic-first extraction plan** you can implement alongside (or ahead of) LLM steps. It is aligned with patterns in your repo (e.g. `business_profiles`, `source_map` / `confidence_map` usage in `market-signal-scorer`) but does not assume a single pipeline. --- ## 1. Pipeline tiers (determinism order) 1. **T0 — Structured inputs**: user form, calibration JSON, CRM row, `business_profiles` existing columns. 2. **T1 — Deterministic parsers**: regex, HTML microdata/JSON-LD, `mailto:`/`tel:`, ABN ACN validators, sitemap/robots hints. 3. **T2 — Rule-based normalizers**: industry taxonomy map, competitor list dedupe, address geocode only as *evidence* (store raw string + provider ref). 4. **T3 — Evidence-bound LLM**: only fills fields when every atomic claim maps to a `EvidenceSpan`; otherwise leave unknown + `ai_errors`. 5. **T4 — Innovation modules** (SWOT, SEO, etc.): same contract as T3; **no free-text without citations**. --- ## 2. Global unknown-marking rules | Situation | Value | Notes | |-----------|--------|------| | Confirmed absent on source | `null` | JSON `null`, not string `"null"` | | Mentioned but not extractable (ambiguous) | `null` + `confidence[field] = 0` + `reason_codes` | Do not guess | | List field with no items | `[]` | Not `null` if schema is array | | Optional string allowed empty | `""` only if product explicitly distinguishes “empty string” | Prefer `null` for “unknown” | | Partial extraction | Store **best substring** + `partial: true` in evidence | Never invent continuation | | Conflicting sources | `null` + `ai_errors[]` entry `CONFLICTING_SOURCES` | Or keep highest-priority tier (T0 > T1 > T3) and log conflict | **Confidence** (per field, 0–1): - `1.0` — T0 user attestation or T1 pattern match with validator pass. - `0.7–0.9` — T1 match without full validator (e.g. phone format OK, country unknown). - `0.3–0.6` — T3 LLM paraphrase with full evidence spans. - `0` — unknown or rejected. --- ## 3. Regex / string-rule strategy by field **Shared primitives** - `EMAIL`: `(?i)[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}` - `PHONE_AU_LOOSE`: `(?:\+?61|0)[\s-]?(?:4\d{2}|[2378]\d{2})[\s-]?\d{3}[\s-]?\d{3}` (+ strip spaces for normalize) - `ABN_SPACES`: `\b(?:\d{2}\s?){5}\d{2}\b` → normalize to 11 digits, **checksum** (mod 89) for acceptance - `URL`: `https?://[^\s"'<>]+` - `SOCIAL_HANDLE`: platform-specific path or `@handle` (Twitter/X, LinkedIn `/company/`, Instagram, Facebook) - `BLOCK_CLEAN`: collapse whitespace, trim, max length per field (e.g. description 4k chars) **Field-specific** | Field | Primary deterministic sources | Rules | |--------|-------------------------------|--------| | `business_name` | ``, `og:site_name`, JSON-LD `Organization.name`, `h1` near logo, legal footer “© X” | Prefer JSON-LD > og > registered name from ABN API; strip “\| Home”, “- Official Site” via suffix regex | | `description` | `meta[name=description]`, `og:description`, first substantial `<p>` block | Take longest of meta if length ≥ 50; else first paragraph cluster | | `industry` | JSON-LD `industry`, NAICS text, keyword map from H1+H2+nav | Map to controlled vocabulary; else `null` | | `main_products_services` | Nav labels, `ItemList` / `Product` JSON-LD, “Services” section headings | Bullet join with `; ` max N items | | `target_market` | Explicit “for …”, “ideal customer”, FAQ | Regex around “we serve”, “target”, “B2B/B2C”; require noun phrase | | `unique_value_proposition` | Hero H1+H2 pair, first CTA section | Often no single regex — T3 only with quotes | | `competitive_advantages` | “Why us”, comparison tables | Extract list items under known headings | | `competitors` | User text, “vs X”, comparison pages, external API | Dedupe by normalized domain/name | | `competitor_analysis` | Structured bullets if present | Mostly T3 evidence-bound | | `market_position` | Pricing page tier, “leader”, “niche” claims | T1 keywords + T3 with citations | | `abn` | Footer, /about, AU regex + checksum | Reject if checksum fails | | `phone` | `tel:` links, footer | Normalize E.164 where country known | | `email` | `mailto:` | Lowercase local part | | `address` | `PostalAddress` JSON-LD, footer block | Keep raw + structured subfields if parsed | | `social_handles` | `a[href*=facebook.com]` etc. | Map platform → handle | | `trust_signals` | Badges (ISO, awards), review widgets, “As seen in” logos | List of `{type, text, url}` from DOM | | `executive_summary` | **Synthesized** | T3 only: max 5 sentences, each sentence ≥1 evidence span | | `confidence` | **Aggregate** | Weighted mean of field confidences used downstream or explicit rubric | | `cmo_executive_brief` | **Synthesized** | T3; template slots filled only from cited facts | | `seo_analysis` | Title length, meta desc presence, H1 count, canonical, robots, sitemap link | Deterministic metrics object + optional T3 interpretation with citations | | `paid_media_analysis` | `ads.txt`, gtag/pixel detection (presence only) | No spend numbers without doc evidence | | `social_media_analysis` | Open Graph completeness, handle consistency | Deterministic checks | | `website_health` | HTTP status, TLS, broken link sample, LCP if you run perf | Mostly tooling output | | `swot` | **Never** from thin air | Each S/W/O/T item = `{text, evidence_spans[]}` | | `competitor_swot` | Per competitor same structure | Same | | `cmo_priority_actions` | Ranked list | Each action `{action, rationale, evidence_spans[], impact_guess ENUM}` — `impact_guess` only if allowed as non-factual enum | --- ## 4. Source evidence mapping format (per field) Use one **canonical envelope**; field-specific payload under `value`. ```json { "field": "business_name", "value": "Acme Pty Ltd", "confidence": 0.95, "unknown": false, "sources": [ { "source_id": "src_001", "tier": "T1", "type": "html_dom | http_header | json_ld | user_input | api_abr | calibration_transcript | pdf_text", "url": "https://example.com/", "fetched_at": "2026-03-28T12:00:00Z", "selector": "script[type='application/ld+json']", "span": { "start": 1024, "end": 1055, "text": "Acme Pty Ltd" }, "hash": "sha256:...", "parser": "jsonld_organization_name_v1" } ], "rules_applied": ["prefer_json_ld_over_og", "abn_checksum_ok"], "partial": false } ``` - **Transcripts**: `span` = `{ "message_id", "char_start", "char_end", "text" }`. - **Arrays** (e.g. `competitors`): each element has its own `sources` or shares parent with per-item spans. - **Objects** (e.g. `seo_analysis`): keys are sub-fields; each sub-field may have nested `sources`. --- ## 5. `ai_errors` format Append-only friendly, machine-readable. ```json { "errors": [ { "code": "MISSING_EVIDENCE | HALLUCINATION_GUARD | PARSE_FAILURE | CONFLICTING_SOURCES | RATE_LIMIT | VALIDATION_FAILED | SCHEMA_MISMATCH", "field": "abn", "message": "ABN checksum failed", "severity": "error | warning | info", "tier": "T1", "details": { "raw": "12 345 678 901", "expected_checksum": false }, "recovery": "null_field" } ], "run_id": "uuid", "model": "optional-model-id", "prompt_version": "extract_v3" } ``` - **HALLUCINATION_GUARD**: model output dropped because a sentence lacked `evidence_spans`. - **RECOVERY** documents what the pipeline did (`null_field`, `kept_t0`, `retried`). --- ## 6. Innovation modules (evidence-bound) | Module | Output shape | Binding rule | |--------|--------------|--------------| | Executive / CMO brief | Sections with `claims[]` | Each claim: `{text, evidence_spans[], confidence}` | | SEO / paid / social analysis | `{metrics: {...}, narrative: string | null}` | If `narrative` non-empty, every clause must link to `metrics` keys or `evidence_spans` | | SWOT / competitor SWOT | `{S:[items], W:[...], ...}` | Each item: `{text, evidence_spans[]}`; optional `competitor_id` for competitor SWOT | | CMO priority actions | Ordered list | `{action, evidence_spans[], dependencies[]}` — no revenue impact numbers without cited data | **Forbidden**: narrative paragraphs with zero spans. **Allowed**: enumerated hypotheses tagged `"hypothesis": true` and **excluded** from `confidence` aggregation for compliance-facing views. --- ## 7. Implementation checklist - [ ] Define JSON schema for extraction result + `source_map` + `confidence_map` + `ai_errors`. - [ ] Implement T1 fetcher (HTML + JSON-LD parser) with content hashing and stable `source_id`. - [ ] Implement shared validators (ABN, email, phone) returning `VALIDATION_FAILED` or normalized value. - [ ] Build per-field **extractor registry** (function list per field, priority order). - [ ] Implement merge policy: T0 → T1 → T3, conflict logging. - [ ] LLM: constrained JSON schema + post-processor that **strips** any claim without matching transcript/DOM span text (fuzzy match window, e.g. ≥85% token overlap). - [ ] Unit tests: golden HTML fixtures per field; ABN edge cases; conflict cases. - [ ] Observability: `run_id`, timings, bytes fetched, truncation flags. - [ ] Privacy: redact PII in stored spans if required by policy. - [ ] Version `parser` and `prompt_version` for reproducibility. --- ## 8. Pseudocode only ```text function extractProfile(job): result = empty ResultSchema source_map = {} confidence_map = {} ai_errors = [] raw_sources = [] raw_sources += load_T0_user_and_db(job.user_id) raw_sources += fetch_primary_pages(job.website) // homepage, /about, /contact, /privacy raw_sources += fetch_calibration_transcript(job.user_id) catalog = buildSourceCatalog(raw_sources) // each chunk: id, url, text, dom, jsonld for field in ALL_FIELDS: candidates = [] for rule in REGISTRY[field]: // ordered: T0 rules, T1 rules, ... try: out = rule.apply(catalog) if out != UNSET: candidates.append(out) catch e: ai_errors.append(makeError(PARSE_FAILURE, field, e)) chosen = mergeByTier(candidates, ai_errors) if chosen.value is UNKNOWN: result[field] = null_or_empty_array(field) confidence_map[field] = 0 else: result[field] = chosen.value source_map[field] = chosen.sources confidence_map[field] = chosen.confidence // T3 modules only where still null or for synthesized fields llm_payload = packageEvidence(catalog, source_map) // truncated, hashed llm_out = callLLM(STRICT_SCHEMA, llm_payload) for field in SYNTHESIZED_FIELDS: // executive_summary, cmo_*, swot narratives, etc. validated, errs = bindEvidence(llm_out[field], catalog) ai_errors.extend(errs) if validated.ok: result[field] = validated.value source_map[field] = validated.sources confidence_map[field] = validated.confidence else: result[field] = null_or_empty_array(field) confidence_map[field] = 0 result.confidence = aggregateConfidence(confidence_map, WEIGHTS) persist(result, source_map, confidence_map, ai_errors, run_id=job.run_id) return result function bindEvidence(moduleOutput, catalog): errors = [] for claim in moduleOutput.allClaims(): if claim.evidence_spans is empty: errors.append(HALLUCINATION_GUARD(claim.field)) return (ok=false, errors) for span in claim.evidence_spans: if not verifySpanAgainstCatalog(span, catalog): errors.append(MISSING_EVIDENCE(...)) return (ok=false, errors) return (ok=true, errors=[]) function mergeByTier(candidates, ai_errors): sort candidates by tier T0 < T1 < T3 group by canonical value (normalize strings) if single group: return best confidence in group if conflict: ai_errors.append(CONFLICTING_SOURCES); return highest tier winner or UNKNOWN ``` --- This gives you a **single deterministic spine** (regex, JSON-LD, validators, tier merge), **explicit unknowns**, **auditable evidence** per field, and **innovation modules** that cannot silently invent facts. If you want this wired to a specific table shape in Supabase (`business_profiles` vs JSON columns), say which storage model you use and we can map `source_map` / `confidence_map` keys 1:1 to columns.
